claim: The quantum error-correction preflight is a replayable local 3-qubit bit-flip stabilizer proof packet: it declares logical states, stabilizers, syndrome bits, correction map, supported error model, emits QEC_STABILIZER_FIXTURE_MATCH for no-error and single Pauli-X errors on both logical basis states, rejects or marks unverifiable five negative controls, and keeps surface-code, hardware QEC, fault-tolerant computation, quantum advantage, cryptographic, and BuildLang/buildc-native claims out of scope.
scope: Project Telos thirtieth-wave prooflesson for proof-carrying quantum-computing claims
decision_summary: The packet verifies the local 3-qubit QEC stabilizer fixture shape and claim boundary. It does not verify surface-code decoding, hardware QEC, fault-tolerant quantum computing, quantum advantage, cryptographic security, or BuildLang/buildc-native runtime execution.
